title: 10 best herbal creams for alzheimer’s disease

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extract is rich in antioxidants that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are believed to contribute to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba extract can improve blood flow to the brain by dilating blood vessels, which may help deliver essential nutrients and oxygen to brain cells that are affected by Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective compounds: Ginkgo biloba extract contains a variety of neuroprotective compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, that have been shown to inhibit the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with alzheimer’s disease because it improves blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and memory. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba creams neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to break down beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, allowing for improved communication between brain cells. This leads to enhanced memory, focus, and mental clarity.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Bilobalide: This constituent may improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's, such as memory loss and cognitive decline.
Ginkgolides: These compounds have been found to have a neuroprotective effect by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory mediators, which can contribute to the development of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow to the brain and enhance cognitive function.
Seeds: They contain ginkgolides, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve memory and cognitive function.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may also improve blood flow to the brain and enhance cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Weigh ginkgo biloba extract 1-2% of total formula weight and store in a cool dry place.
Mix ginkgo biloba extract with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil 10-15% in a clean glass beaker.
Add beeswax 5-7% and emulsifying wax 2-3% to the mixture and melt in a double boiler.
Gradually add distilled water 15-20% to the melted wax mixture and blend with a hand mixer.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and let it cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Nootropic and Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi cream contains compounds like brahmin, apigenin, and ursolic acid that have been shown to improve cognitive function, memory, and concentration by enhancing the communication between neurons in the brain.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and prevent neuronal death, which are key mechanisms underlying Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective and Neuroregenerative Properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds have been shown to prevent or slow down the progression of neurodegeneration in Alzheimer's disease by promoting the growth and differentiation of neurons, as well as reducing the accumulation of amyloid-beta plaques and tau tangles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri, also known as brahmi, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing properties. The cream is absorbed through the skin, allowing the active compounds to penetrate the brain and stimulate the growth of new neurons. This can help improve memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Bacopa monnieri has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are common underlying factors in the progression of the disease.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of triterpene saponins found in Bacopa monnieri, which have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing the expression of neurotrophic factors and protecting against neuronal damage.

Bacopa glycosides: Bacopa glycosides are a type of flavonoid glycoside found in Bacopa monnieri, which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the brain, thus helping to prevent the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Bacopaside II: Bacopaside II is a specific bacoside that has been shown to have a positive effect on memory and cognitive function by inhibiting the activity of acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, which is involved in learning and memory processes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, including bacosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective properties.
Roots: They are used as they contain a higher concentration of bacosides compared to other parts, making them a key ingredient for Alzheimer's treatment.
Stems: They are used due to their rich content of alkaloids and glycosides, which are thought to enhance memory and cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems from a pesticide-free area for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 200 grams of dried bacopa monnieri into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 2 minutes.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and mix in 50 grams of beeswax for a smooth consistency.
Combine 20 grams of the powder with 50 grams of the melted oil mixture and whip until it thickens into a cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Golden Root contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to cognitive decline and neurodegeneration.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Golden Root extracts have been shown to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in memory formation and consolidation, potentially improving cognitive function.
Inflammation reduction: Golden Root's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is thought to play a key role in Alzheimer's disease pathogenesis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea, also known as golden root, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potential neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Rhodiola rosea, such as rosavin and salidroside, may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea creams may improve memory and cognitive function by increasing the production of neurotransmitters and enhancing blood flow to the brain, making it a promising natural remedy for managing Alzheimer's disease symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: Rosavin is a phenolic glycoside found in Rhodiola rosea that has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory in individuals with Alzheimer's disease by increasing the activity of neurotransmitters and enhancing blood flow to the brain.

Salidroside: Salidroside is a phenolic compound found in Rhodiola rosea that has been demonstrated to have neuroprotective effects, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Tyrosol: Tyrosol is a phenolic compound found in Rhodiola rosea that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the progression of Alzheimer's disease by protecting brain cells from damage and promoting the clearance of amyloid beta plaques.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high levels of rosmarinic acid and salidroside, which are believed to have neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing effects.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to improve memory and cognitive function by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Stems: They contain adaptogenic compounds, such as rosavin and rosin, which may help to reduce stress and anxiety, and improve overall cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried rhodiola rosea root with antioxidant properties and store in an airtight container for future use.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax and 25 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of distilled water to the melted mixture and stir well.
Mix 5 grams of glycerin and 2 grams of panthenol into the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers and allow to cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Herbal sage creams contain compounds that inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter crucial for memory and learning, which can help slow down cognitive decline in people with Alzheimer's.

Antioxidant activity: Sage cream's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

GABA enhancement: Some herbal sage creams contain GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate neural activity and calmness, which can help reduce anxiety and agitation associated with Alzheimer's.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with alzheimer’s disease because it contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, sage creams may improve cognitive function and memory by increasing the production of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in memory formation and retrieval.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Urosolic acid: A triterpenoid saponin with neuroprotective properties, urosolic acid may help reduce beta-amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles in the brain, contributing to the progression of Alzheimer's.

Salvianolic acid A: A phenolic acid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, salvianolic acid A may help protect neuronal cells from damage and reduce the risk of neurodegeneration in Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, a compound that may help improve memory and cognitive function.
Roots: The roots are used for their potential to improve cognitive function and memory due to the presence of bioactive compounds that may have neuroprotective effects.
Buds: The buds are also used for their potential cognitive benefits, possibly due to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds that may help protect against oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency and dry them completely within 24 hours.
Steep the dried herbs in 100ml of carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container to separate solids.
Mix 20-30 grams of beeswax and 20-30 grams of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and reaches a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for application to the skin.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.

Membrane-stabilizing effects: Rosemary's compounds have been shown to stabilize and preserve the integrity of brain cell membranes, potentially helping to prevent damage caused by Alzheimer's-related changes.

Neuroprotective flavonoids: Rosemary contains flavonoids like rosmarinic acid, which may help protect against neuronal death, reduce amyloid-β plaque accumulation, and improve cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in rosemary creams may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, rosemary's ability to improve blood flow and boost the growth of new neurons may also contribute to its potential benefits in managing Alzheimer's symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: A polyphenolic diterpene that has neuroprotective properties, which help prevent the formation of amyloid plaques and reduce oxidative stress, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the production of beta-amyloid peptides and prevent neuronal damage, ultimately slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Rosavin (Rosmarinic acid precursor): A polyphenolic compound that is converted to rosmarinic acid in the body, and has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are commonly used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which has neuroprotective properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are also used, as they contain a higher concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help reduce oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are used due to their potential to inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Infuse the herbs in 150ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining the liquid oil.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 30g of the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Effects: The calming and soothing properties of lavender may help alleviate anxiety, agitation, and insomnia associated with Alzheimer's disease, improving overall quality of life for patients and caregivers.

Improvement in Cognitive Function: The essential oils present in lavender cream, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a positive impact on cognitive function, including improved memory, attention, and processing speed.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of the disease. The calming effects of lavender may also help manage agitation and anxiety associated with Alzheimer's, promoting a better quality of life for patients. Its potential therapeutic benefits make it a promising adjunctive treatment.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia, has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate, another terpene present in Lavandula angustifolia, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease, such as memory loss and cognitive decline.

Lavandulol: Lavandulol, a terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia, has been demonstrated to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against neurodegeneration and improve cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and neuroprotective properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with the disease.
Seeds: They are used due to their potential to improve cognitive function and memory, as well as their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a clean and organic source.
Steep the dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container.
Blend 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter with 4 ounces of infused oil.
Whip the mixture until it thickens to a desired consistency for a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Maypop cream contains antioxidants like flavonoids and anthocyanins that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Cholinesterase inhibition: Maypop has been shown to inhibit cholinesterase enzymes, which breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter important for memory and cognitive function. By inhibiting these enzymes, maypop cream may help increase levels of acetylcholine in the brain, potentially improving memory and cognitive function.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Maypop contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and kaempferol that can help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is thought to play a role in the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains flavonoids, such as flavonoid homoisoflavonoids, that have neuroprotective properties. These compounds can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, the sedative and anxiolytic effects of Passiflora incarnata can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which may be beneficial for individuals with Alzheimer's disease and their caregivers.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly flavonols and flavanols, in Passiflora incarnata have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Alkaloids (Isoquinoline alkaloids): Alkaloids, particularly harmine and harmaline, have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially helping to prevent the progression of Alzheimer's disease by blocking beta-amyloid aggregation and reducing neuroinflammation.

Terpenoids (Rotundifuranes): Rotundifuranes, a type of terpenoid found in Passiflora incarnata, have demonstrated antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to the prevention of cognitive decline and memory loss associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potential neuroprotective effects.
Fruits: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Seeds: They are used for their potential neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the management of Alzheimer's disease symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried passiflora incarnata flowers with a sharp gardening tool in early morning sunlight.
Mix 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of coconut oil in a large glass jar for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 48 hours of steeping time.
Add 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to the coconut oil mixture and heat it.
Pour the cream mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hops contain powerful antioxidants like humulone, which can help protect brain cells from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, potentially slowing down cognitive decline.

Inflammation reduction: Hops have anti-inflammatory compounds like xanthohumol, which may help reduce inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease, thereby improving cognitive function and memory.

Amyloid-β aggregation inhibition: The alpha-acid content in hops has been shown to inhibit the aggregation of amyloid-β peptides, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, potentially slowing down or halting its progression.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus, also known as hops, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extracts from Humulus lupulus have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are key factors contributing to the progression of Alzheimer's. The cream's ability to penetrate the skin and reach the brain may help alleviate symptoms such as memory loss and cognitive decline. Additionally, its neuroprotective properties may help prevent neuronal damage, making it a promising natural remedy for Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Lupulone: Lupulone, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Humulene: Humulene, a sesquiterpene, has been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect neurons from damage and reduce the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide, a sesquiterpene, has been reported to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the production of amyloid beta plaques and tau protein tangles associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: They are used because of their possible neuroprotective effects, which could help protect brain cells from damage associated with Alzheimer's.
Buds: They are used due to their potential to stimulate the brain's natural defense mechanisms and promote healthy cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried humulus lupulus flowers at the peak of their potency and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 100g of the harvested flowers with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass container.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 200ml of the infused oil in a double boiler and heat the mixture until the wax melts.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool then whip the cream until it thickens to the desired consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian cream helps reduce inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease by damaging brain cells and disrupting cognitive function.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage brain tissue and contribute to Alzheimer's symptoms such as memory loss and cognitive decline.

GABA-boosting properties: Valerian has been shown to increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, anxiety, and relaxation, which can be disrupted in people with Alzheimer's disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potential to improve sleep quality, a crucial factor in managing the condition. Valerian root has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia and anxiety commonly associated with Alzheimer's. The cream's calming properties may also enhance cognitive function and memory by promoting a restful night's sleep, ultimately contributing to a better quality of life for those affected. This natural approach may offer a supportive treatment option for individuals with Alzheimer's.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and neuroprotectant, helping to prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Valerenal: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, which may help alleviate stress and anxiety that can contribute to cognitive decline and memory loss.
Valeranone: Shows potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, a key factor in the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in valerenic acid, which is believed to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: Valerian leaves contain valerenal, a compound that may have a positive effect on cognitive function and memory by modulating neurotransmitter activity.
Barks: Valerian barks are a source of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried valeriana officinalis roots from a reputable supplier and store them in an airtight container.
Mix 50g of the dried valeriana officinalis roots with 500ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 20 minutes then reduce heat to low and let it steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids then add 20g of beeswax to the liquid.
Heat the mixture to 180f and whisk until the beeswax has melted then pour it into a glass jar and let it cool.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginseng creams contain antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which are known to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective effects: The ginsenosides in ginseng creams have been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which may help prevent or slow down the degeneration of brain cells associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Cognitive-enhancing compounds: Ginseng creams contain compounds that have been found to improve cognitive function and memory, which can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease such as memory loss and confusion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains compounds that may improve cognitive function and boost memory. The cream's ginsenosides have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are associated with the development of Alzheimer's. Additionally, ginseng creams may help increase blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neural function and potentially slowing the progression of the disease.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides have neuroprotective effects, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Ginsenolic acid: Ginsenolic acid has been shown to inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques, a key hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and also has antioxidant properties to protect neurons from damage.

Saponins: Saponins, particularly ginsenoside Rg1, have been found to improve cognitive function and reduce the risk of Alzheimer's disease by modulating neurotransmitter levels and enhancing neuronal survival.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used due to its high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective properties.
Leaves: The secondary part used for its flavonoid content, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: The tertiary part used for its potential to enhance cognitive function due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of beeswax and 500 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt.
Steep 100 grams of dried panax ginseng root in 1 liter of boiling water for 30 minutes to infuse.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids to separate.
Combine the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture with the ginseng infusion in a large mixing bowl.
Stir the mixture constantly as it cools and thickens over the course of 1 hour to emulsify.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with Alzheimer's disease because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to increase blood flow to the brain and improve cognitive function. The cream is absorbed through the skin and reaches the brain, where it may help alleviate symptoms such as memory loss, confusion, and mood changes. Additionally, St John's Wort has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. By addressing these underlying causes, St John's Wort creams may offer a natural and effective way to support brain health.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Flavonoids: St. John's Wort contains flavonoids like hyperoside and quercetin, which have been found to have neuroprotective properties, including the ability to inhibit amyloid-β aggregation, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
Phenolic acids: These compounds may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which could contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Buds: The buds contain hypericin, a flavonoid that has been found to improve cognitive function and memory by increasing blood flow to the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried st. john's wort flowers and leaves according to good manufacturing practices.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 30 grams of powdered hypericum perforatum with 150 ml of sweet almond oil in a glass jar and let it infuse for 14 days.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids before adding 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil to the liquid.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally, then pour into sterilized tubes or jars and allow to cool and harden completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain parthenolide, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines in the brain. This can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's. By reducing these harmful processes, feverfew creams may help slow down or even halt the advancement of cognitive decline associated with the disease, improving symptoms such as memory loss and confusion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Feverfew contains parthenolide, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with various health conditions.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall health.

Neuroprotective effects: Some studies suggest that feverfew may have neuroprotective properties, potentially helping to reduce the risk of neurological disorders such as Alzheimer's disease, although more research is needed to confirm this.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain a high concentration of parthenolide, a bioactive compound shown to have neuroprotective properties, making them suitable for Alzheimer's disease treatment.

Flowers: Feverfew flowers possess a unique combination of flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help mitigate the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers, ensuring proper identification and authentication.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or vacuum drying for 2-3 hours at 60°c.
Combine 250 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Macerate the mixture for 48 hours, shaking occasionally, and then strain through cheesecloth to separate solid and liquid components.
Mix the filtered liquid with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil, heating at 60°c for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with Alzheimer’s disease because they possess a unique blend of compounds that can alleviate symptoms such as memory loss, anxiety, and depression. The calming properties of lemon balm cream may help reduce agitation and aggression in patients with the disease. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may help slow down cognitive decline by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. By promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, lemon balm creams can also support overall brain health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Cholinergic activity: Lemon balm contains compounds that stimulate cholinergic receptors in the brain, enhancing memory and cognitive function by increasing the levels of neurotransmitters such as acetylcholine.

Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm cream has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, improving overall mental well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are valued for their ability to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation, making them useful in creams that aim to alleviate cognitive decline and memory loss associated with Alzheimer's.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and improving cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves ensuring freshness and potency for optimal extraction.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 8 hours or oven at 60°c for 3 hours.
Combine dried melissa officinalis leaves with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:5 and steep for 4 weeks at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids before adding beeswax to achieve a 10% concentration for optimal cream formation.
Heat the mixture gently under low heat until the beeswax melts completely and the cream thickens within 15-20 minutes of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess potent neuroprotective properties. These compounds, including sesquiterpenes and saponins, have been found to cross the blood-brain barrier, reaching the brain and interacting with damaged neurons to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. By reducing these underlying factors contributing to cognitive decline, Asiatic pennywort creams may help slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's disease, offering new hope for those affected by this devastating condition.
remedy_properties: Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Asian Pennywort extracts have been shown to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory and cognitive function, thus helping to improve memory and cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's.

Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease and its symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Asian Pennywort has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, thereby potentially slowing down or halting cognitive decline.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used for their potential neuroprotective and antioxidant properties, making them effective in combating cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's.

Rhyzomes: With a high concentration of polysaccharides and other bioactive molecules, rhyzomes have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and dry them in shade for 7-10 days.
Grind 100g of dried centella asiatica leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 30g of ground centella asiatica powder with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in double boiler for 1 hour.
Stir mixture until it reaches temperature of 65°c to 70°c and then remove from heat for 30 minutes.
Fill sterilized cream jars with cooled mixture and store them in refrigerator for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to slow down cognitive decline. Curcumin crosses the blood-brain barrier, where it can target amyloid plaques and tau tangles, two hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, turmeric creams may help alleviate symptoms such as memory loss, confusion, and mood disturbances associated with the disease. Additionally, curcumin has been found to promote neurogenesis, the growth of new neurons, which can lead to improved cognitive function.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, a key factor in Alzheimer's disease.

Bioavailability-enhancing agents: Herbal creams containing bioavailability-enhancing agents like phosphatidylcholine or lecithin can increase the absorption of curcumin into the bloodstream and brain, allowing it to reach its therapeutic targets more effectively.

Brain-permeable compounds: Turmeric creams often contain additional compounds that are specifically designed to penetrate the blood-brain barrier, such as turmerone or ar-turmerone, which can directly target and reduce beta-amyloid plaque formation in the brain, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is the underground stem that gives rise to new plants, contains curcuminoids and other bioactive compounds that have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for Alzheimer's disease.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function, making them a key component in medicinal creams for Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 10 grams of ground turmeric with 20 ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to allow turmeric extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 2 grams of beeswax and 5 ml of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for application on skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, common hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been found to stimulate blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuron function and reducing the risk of neurodegeneration associated with this devastating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective properties: The bioactive compounds present in herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams have been shown to inhibit the formation of amyloid beta plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and may help protect against neuronal damage.

Improving insulin sensitivity: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams may improve insulin sensitivity, which is often compromised in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, helping to regulate blood sugar levels and mitigate cognitive decline.
remedy_parts: Barks: Contain cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help prevent the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Rich in phenolic compounds, including coumarin, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered cinnamomum verum with 200 milliliters of warm distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container to collect the liquid extract.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil with the cinnamomum verum extract in a double boiler, heating until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool slightly, then pour it into sterilized containers and allow it to solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with Alzheimer’s disease because they contain the herb Angelica sinensis, which has been traditionally used to promote blood flow and balance hormones in the body. The cream may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are associated with Alzheimer’s disease. Additionally, dong quai is believed to improve cognitive function by enhancing neurotransmitter production and communication between brain cells. By promoting healthy brain function, dong quai creams may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's such as memory loss and confusion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Dong quai has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.

Neuroprotective effects: Some studies suggest that dong quai may have neuroprotective effects, possibly by increasing the production of neurotransmitters like acetylcholine or reducing the accumulation of beta-amyloid plaques in the brain.
Cognitive-enhancing properties: Dong quai has been traditionally used to improve memory and cognitive function, although this effect is not well-studied scientifically.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of Dong Quai have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the plant, which contains a high concentration of volatile oils that can help to reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including angelica sinensis root powder, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin e oil, and distilled water.
Combine 100g of angelica sinensis root powder with 200ml of distilled water in a double boiler and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids, then add 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax and stir until melted.
Add 5ml of vitamin e oil and mix well, then pour the mixture into glass jars and store in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours.
Allow the cream to mature for 2-3 days before using, allowing the ingredients to combine and thicken naturally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain kavalactones, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and neuroprotective properties. These compounds have been found to help reduce inflammation in the brain, a common hallmark of Alzheimer's, while also promoting the growth of new neurons and improving cognitive function. By targeting multiple pathways involved in the progression of the disease, herbal kava creams may help slow down or even halt the development of symptoms associated with Alzheimer's, offering hope for a more effective treatment option.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Kava may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which could potentially slow down or halt the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Neuroprotective effects: Kava's flavonoids and lignans may have neuroprotective properties that help preserve brain cells and prevent damage caused by free radicals or other toxic substances.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Kava has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and restlessness, which are common symptoms of Alzheimer's disease. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, kava may help improve sleep quality and overall well-being in individuals with the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: Kava roots contain a high concentration of kavalactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them beneficial in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's.
Barks: The barks of the kava plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been found to have neuroprotective effects, helping to prevent damage to neurons and promote overall brain health.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality piper methysticum roots and carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil.
Dry the piper methysticum roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 100g of dried piper methysticum roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered piper methysticum with 200ml of carrier oil in a double boiler, heating it slowly over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil and herbs, discarding the solids and transferring the cream to an amber glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a compound called baicalin, which has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. Baicalin is believed to cross the blood-brain barrier and reduce inflammation in the brain, two key factors in the development of Alzheimer's. By reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the production of amyloid-β peptides, skullcap creams may help slow down or even stop the progression of the disease. Additionally, baicalin has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory in patients with Alzheimer's, making it a promising natural remedy for this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid found in skullcap, has been shown to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Scutellarein: Scutellarein, another flavonoid in skullcap, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect neurons from damage and improve cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Tannins: Tannins in skullcap have been found to inhibit the activity of acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory and cognition, potentially helping to preserve cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have neuroprotective properties that can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including saponins and phenolic acids, that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest scutellaria lateriflora flowers at dawn in mid-summer when they are in full bloom and dry them immediately.
Combine 20% dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers with 80% organic coconut oil and blend for 30 minutes to create a uniform mixture.
Add 5% beeswax pellets to the mixture and heat it gently at 70°c to 80°c for 10 minutes until melted.
Pour the warm mixture into glass containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature over 2 hours.
Store the scutellaria lateriflora creams in airtight containers in a cool dark place and use within 6 months of preparation.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for alzheimer’s disease are topical products infused with natural herbs and essential oils that help alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. These creams, such as those containing rosemary, peppermint, and lavender, have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. For instance, rosemary cream has been found to enhance memory and concentration, while peppermint cream has been shown to improve mood and reduce anxiety. By using these creams, individuals with Alzheimer's can experience improved quality of life and enhanced cognitive function.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to Alzheimer's disease that are treated with herbal creams are neurodegenerative disorders such as dementia, Parkinson's disease, and multiple sclerosis. These conditions share similarities with Alzheimer's in terms of cognitive decline and memory loss. Herbal creams containing ingredients like Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Ashwagandha have been found to improve cognitive function, boost brain health, and alleviate symptoms of these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: I can't provide information on the best combination of herbal creams to heal Alzheimer's disease.
